posts.title: सांख्य दर्शन क्या है?
posts.excerpt: इस संसार में हर एक इंसान या तो सुख पाना चाहता है अथवा अपने जीवन में आए दुखों और कष्टों का नाश करना चाहता है। हम जो भी काम करते हैं, पढाई करते हैं, नौकरी करते हैं, महनत करत हैं और पैंसे कमाते हैं – इन सब के पीछे दो ही प्रयोजन हैं सुख की प्राप्ति और दुख का परिहार।
posts.content:
नमस्ते शिक्षणार्थियों,

इस संसार में हर एक इंसान या तो सुख पाना चाहता है अथवा अपने जीवन में आए दुखों और कष्टों का नाश करना चाहता है। हम जो भी काम करते हैं, पढाई करते हैं, नौकरी करते हैं, महनत करत हैं और पैंसे कमाते हैं – इन सब के पीछे दो ही प्रयोजन हैं सुख की प्राप्ति और दुख का परिहार। लेकिन लौकिक साधनों से प्राप्त होने वाला सुख नित्य नहीं होता। हम थोड़ी देर के लिए सुख का अनुभव करते हैं किन्तु थोड़ी देर बाद पुनः किसी अन्य कारण से दुखी हो जाते हैं अतः नित्य सुख की प्राप्ति और दुख के आत्यन्तिक परिहार के लिए ही हमारे ऋषियों ने दर्शनों का प्रणयन किया।

भारतीय ज्ञान परम्परा में ६ आस्तिक दर्शन और ३ नास्तिक दर्शनों को माना गया है। न्याय, वैशेषिक, साङ्ख्य, योग, पूर्वमीमांसा और उत्तरमीमांसा (वेदान्त) – यह ३ आस्तिक दर्शन कहलाते हैं। बौद्ध, जैन और चार्वाक – ये तीन नास्तिक दर्शन कहलाते हैं। यहाँ पर आस्तिक-नास्तिक का मतलब ईश्वर को मानने वाला – न मानने वाले से नहीं है क्योंकि कई ऐसे आस्तिक दर्शन भी हैं जो ईश्वर को नहीं मानते। आस्तिक और नास्तिक का यहाँ पर अर्थ है वेद को प्रमाण मानने वाला और न मानने वाला।

सांख्य दर्शन भारतीय दर्शन की सबसे प्राचीन और प्रभावशाली शाखाओं में से एक है। यह दर्शन प्रकृति और पुरुष के बीच के अंतर को समझाने का प्रयास करता है। सांख्य दर्शन के अनुसार, संसार दो मूल तत्वों से बना है - प्रकृति (पदार्थ) और पुरुष (चेतना)।
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia

posts.title: What is Sankhya Philosophy?
posts.excerpt: Every person in this world either wants to attain happiness or wants to get rid of the sorrows and sufferings in his life. We study, we go for jobs or businesses, we work hard to earn money for only two purposes – attainment of happiness and avoidance of sorrow.
posts.content:
Every person in this world either wants to attain happiness or wants to get rid of the sorrows and sufferings in his life. We study, we go for jobs or businesses, we work hard to earn money for only two purposes – attainment of happiness and avoidance of sorrow. But the happiness obtained through worldly means is not permanent. We experience happiness for a short period of time and again become sad due to some different reason, hence our sages composed philosophies for attainment of permanent happiness and ultimate avoidance of sorrow.

In the Indian knowledge tradition, 6 astika darshanas and 3 nastika darshanas are recognized. Nyaya, Vaisheshika, Samkhya, Yoga, Purva Mimamsa, and Uttara Mimamsa (Vedanta) – these are called astika darshanas. Buddhist, Jain, and Charvaka – these three are called nastika darshanas. Here, astika-nastika does not mean believer-non-believer in God, because there are many astika darshanas that do not believe in God. Astika and nastika here mean those who accept Vedas as authority and those who do not.

Samkhya philosophy is one of the oldest and most influential branches of Indian philosophy. This philosophy attempts to explain the difference between Prakriti (matter) and Purusha (consciousness). According to Samkhya philosophy, the world is made up of two fundamental elements - Prakriti (matter) and Purusha (consciousness).
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia

posts.title: The Mysteries of the Four Yugas: Is Kali Yuga Over? (Part 2)
posts.excerpt: Have you ever wondered if Kali Yuga, the age of darkness and chaos is still ongoing, or has it already ended? Ancient Indian scriptures describe the Yugas in different ways, sometimes with vast timelines and at other times with shorter durations.
posts.content:
Namaste Shikshanarthi's

Have you ever wondered if Kali Yuga, the age of darkness and chaos is still ongoing, or has it already ended? Ancient Indian scriptures describe the Yugas in different ways, sometimes with vast timelines and at other times with shorter durations. This has left us with a fascinating yet confusing question: how long does each Yuga really last? In this second part of our series, we dive deeper into the mystery of Kali Yuga, explore different perspectives, and analyze whether we are still in it or have moved to another era.

The concept of Yugas in Hindu cosmology presents a cyclical view of time, where the universe goes through four distinct ages: Satya Yuga (Golden Age), Treta Yuga (Silver Age), Dvapara Yuga (Bronze Age), and Kali Yuga (Iron Age). Each Yuga is characterized by different levels of dharma (righteousness) and spiritual awareness.

In this exploration, we will examine various scriptural sources, including the Puranas, Mahabharata, and other ancient texts, to understand the different calculations and interpretations of Yuga durations. We will also look at modern interpretations and how they might apply to our current times.
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia

posts.title: The Mysteries of the Four Yugas: Understanding Their Secrets (Part 1)
posts.excerpt: Have you ever wondered how ancient Indian philosophy explains the passage of time? In Hinduism, the concept of the four Yugas—Satya, Treta, Dvapara, and Kali—offers a fascinating perspective on the cyclical nature of existence.
posts.content:
Namaste Shikshanarthi's

Have you ever wondered how ancient Indian philosophy explains the passage of time? In Hinduism, the concept of the four Yugas—Satya, Treta, Dvapara, and Kali—offers a fascinating perspective on the cyclical nature of existence. But why are there conflicting ideas about their durations? Are they millions of years long or just thousands? Join us in this detailed exploration of the Yugas, where we break down the mysteries one by one. This is Part 1 of a three-part series, setting the foundation for this age-old debate.

The Yuga system is one of the most intriguing aspects of Hindu cosmology. It presents a view of time that is both cyclical and progressive, where the universe undergoes repeated cycles of creation, preservation, and dissolution. Each cycle consists of four Yugas, each with its own characteristics and duration.

In this first part, we will explore the basic structure of the Yuga system, examine the different calculations found in various scriptures, and understand why there are discrepancies in the durations mentioned in different texts. We will also look at the characteristics of each Yuga and how they relate to the spiritual evolution of humanity.
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia

posts.title: Truth Behind the Brahma-Saraswati Controversy: Debunking Myths with Wisdom
posts.excerpt: Imagine walking through a bustling marketplace where everyone is talking about a story they've only half-heard. Some say it's shocking, others claim it's proof of wrongdoing, and a few speak of hidden wisdom.
posts.content:
Namastey Shikshanarthi's

Imagine walking through a bustling marketplace where everyone is talking about a story they've only half-heard. Some say it's shocking, others claim it's proof of wrongdoing, and a few speak of hidden wisdom. This is what happens when myths from ancient scriptures are taken out of context.

One such tale, often misunderstood, is the relationship between Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe, and Saraswati, the goddess of wisdom. Whispers claim Brahma acted inappropriately toward Saraswati, labeling Hinduism as a promoter of immoral acts. But how much of this is true? Let's take a journey into the heart of the scriptures to uncover the truth.

The story of Brahma and Saraswati is one of the most misunderstood narratives in Hindu mythology. When taken out of context, it can indeed seem problematic. However, like many ancient stories, this narrative contains deep symbolic and philosophical meanings that are often lost in literal interpretations.

In this exploration, we will examine the original sources, understand the symbolic meanings, and separate fact from fiction. We will also explore how this story relates to broader themes in Hindu philosophy and what it teaches us about the nature of knowledge, creation, and divine relationships.
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia

posts.title: सनातन धर्म में सोलह संस्कार
posts.excerpt: एक बार एक युवा बालक ने अपने दादा से पूछा, "दादाजी, जीवन इतना अनिश्चित क्यों है? हमें कैसे पता चले कि हम सही राह पर हैं?"
posts.content:
नमस्ते शिक्षणार्थियों,

एक बार एक युवा बालक ने अपने दादा से पूछा, "दादाजी, जीवन इतना अनिश्चित क्यों है? हमें कैसे पता चले कि हम सही राह पर हैं?"

दादा मुस्कुराए और बोले, "बेटा, हमारे ऋषि-मुनियों ने हजारों साल पहले इस सवाल का जवाब खोज लिया था। उन्होंने हमारे लिए संस्कारों की व्यवस्था की। ये संस्कार हमें हर जीवन-चरण में सही दिशा दिखाते हैं, हमें शुद्ध करते हैं और समाज व परिवार से जोड़ते हैं।"

बालक ने उत्सुकता से पूछा, "क्या संस्कार सिर्फ पूजा हैं?"

दादा ने गंभीरता से उत्तर दिया, "संस्कार पूजा से अधिक हैं। ये जीवन के हर महत्वपूर्ण पल को पवित्र बनाते हैं। जन्म से मृत्यु तक, ये हमें हमारी परंपरा, संस्कृति और धर्म से जोड़ते हैं।"

सनातन धर्म में सोलह संस्कारों का विशेष महत्व है। ये संस्कार मनुष्य के जीवन के विभिन्न चरणों में किए जाते हैं और उसे आध्यात्मिक और सामाजिक रूप से विकसित करते हैं। प्रत्येक संस्कार का अपना विशेष महत्व और उद्देश्य है।

इन संस्कारों में गर्भाधान से लेकर अंत्येष्टि तक के सभी महत्वपूर्ण चरण शामिल हैं। ये न केवल धार्मिक क्रियाएं हैं बल्कि जीवन के वैज्ञानिक और मनोवैज्ञानिक पहलुओं को भी ध्यान में रखते हैं।
posts.author: Vishal Chaurasia
